behaviors.description: The clause extractor identifies all key terms (parties, dates, obligations, penalties) present in the contract.
behaviors.justification: LLM output quality determines correctness, and extraction can vary by phrasing and run-to-run model behavior. A mock extractor would hide misses on ambiguous legal text, so Unit/Integration would not reliably catch real failures.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real clause-extractor model on a curated set of real contracts with gold annotations for parties/dates/obligations/penalties, including edge phrasing. Execute repeated runs and fail if recall drops below threshold or required term classes are missed.

behaviors.description: The risk assessor flags language that would be problematic under the configured risk policy, even when the problematic phrasing is indirect or euphemistic.
behaviors.justification: Detecting indirect/euphemistic risk language depends on real model interpretation, not deterministic rules alone. Mocked responses would likely pass and miss semantic failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real risk-assessor model against a benchmark of policy-tagged clauses containing direct and euphemistic risk language. Require detection of policy-defined risks with minimum precision/recall, and include repeated trials to expose model variance.

behaviors.description: The compliance checker correctly determines whether the contract meets all applicable regulatory requirements for the configured jurisdiction.
behaviors.justification: Jurisdictional compliance judgment is semantic and model-dependent; real failures come from interpretation errors on legal text. Pure mocks can mask those errors.
behaviors.test_description: Run the real compliance checker for each target jurisdiction on contracts with expert-labeled compliance outcomes and cited requirement mappings. Fail on incorrect pass/fail decisions or missing required citations beyond threshold; include repeated runs for stability.

behaviors.description: When two reviewers flag the same clause with conflicting assessments, the merge step preserves both assessments with attribution.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is schema/wiring between reviewer outputs and merge logic. A unit test of merge internals can miss cross-module contract mismatches; no real LLM call is required.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke the actual reviewer-output parsers/DTOs plus merge module with conflicting clause assessments from two reviewers (and optional third reviewer noise). Verify merged report keeps both assessments, correct reviewer attribution, and no conflict overwrite.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline dispatches all three reviewers, collects results, and merges into a unified report without losing findings.
behaviors.justification: This is explicitly a multi-step pipeline with parallel agentic reviewers followed by merge. Real failures include orchestration races, partial collection, and cross-step loss that lower-level tests miss.
behaviors.test_description: Execute the end-to-end pipeline on real contracts using all three real reviewers in parallel, then merge. Assert each reviewer completed, all per-reviewer findings are present in final report, conflicts are retained with attribution, and no findings are dropped under repeated runs.
